front.title: Meta-Learning Frameworks
front.subtitle: Advanced systems designed to help artificial intelligence learn how to learn from limited data.

back.text:
Meta-learning frameworks represent a shift in artificial intelligence from task-specific training to general adaptability. While traditional machine learning requires thousands of examples to master a single skill, meta-learning enables models to draw upon past experiences to solve new problems with only a handful of data points. This process, often called few-shot learning, mimics the human ability to recognize a new object or concept after seeing it only once or twice.

These frameworks function by optimizing the learning process itself. During the meta-training phase, the system is exposed to a variety of different tasks, allowing it to develop a set of internal priors or initialization weights. When faced with a novel challenge, the model uses these pre-optimized starting points to rapidly fine-tune its parameters. Popular frameworks like Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML) and Prototypical Networks have become foundational in fields where data is scarce, such as medical imaging and personalized robotics.
